seo.title: Semaglutide in the UK — Wegovy, Ozempic, NHS Access and Private Cost
seo.description: MHRA-approved semaglutide pathways in the UK. How Wegovy and Ozempic are accessed on the NHS and privately, what NICE TA875 means for eligible patients, and what private prescriptions currently cost.

trust.disclaimer: This page summarises UK-specific semaglutide access based on publicly available MHRA, NICE, and NHS data. Product availability, NHS eligibility criteria, and private pricing change frequently. Always verify current status with your GP or a UK-licensed prescriber.

marketSummary: In the United Kingdom, semaglutide is an MHRA-regulated prescription medicine available as Wegovy (weight management) and Ozempic (type 2 diabetes). NHS access exists through a NICE-approved pathway with eligibility criteria. Private prescribing is widely available from UK-licensed providers.
approvalScope: Weight management (Wegovy) and type 2 diabetes (Ozempic, Rybelsus) — MHRA approved

approvedProducts.name: Wegovy
approvedProducts.summary: Semaglutide 2.4 mg subcutaneous injection MHRA-approved for chronic weight management in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities. NICE-recommended (TA875) through specialist weight management pathways.

approvedProducts.name: Ozempic
approvedProducts.summary: Semaglutide 0.5 mg, 1 mg, and 2 mg subcutaneous injection MHRA-approved for type 2 diabetes. Available on NHS prescription under standard T2D prescribing.

approvedProducts.name: Rybelsus
approvedProducts.summary: Oral semaglutide tablet MHRA-approved for type 2 diabetes. An oral alternative for patients who prefer not to self-inject.

costSummary: Private Wegovy prescriptions in the UK typically range from £175 to £350 per month depending on dose and provider. NHS access through the NICE-approved pathway is substantially lower cost but requires eligibility and a specialist referral. Ozempic is available on NHS under standard T2D prescriptions at the standard NHS prescription charge.
providerPathway: UK patients can access semaglutide via NHS GP referral into a specialist weight management service (NICE TA875 pathway for Wegovy) or via private telehealth and in-person providers. Multiple CQC-registered UK telehealth platforms offer private Wegovy and Ozempic prescribing with online consultations.
onlinePharmacyNotes: Online prescribing is legal in the UK when delivered by a CQC-registered service using MHRA-licensed prescribers. Patients should confirm that any online provider and dispensing pharmacy holds current CQC and GPhC registration before purchasing.
legalNotes: Semaglutide requires a valid UK prescription. Purchasing without a prescription or from unlicensed sources is illegal.
Direct-to-consumer advertising of prescription-only medicines is prohibited under the Human Medicines Regulations 2012.
Supply shortages have periodically affected Wegovy and Ozempic UK availability. The MHRA publishes current shortage information on GOV.UK.

faqs.question: Can I get Wegovy on the NHS in the UK?
faqs.answer: Yes, but through a restricted pathway. NICE approved Wegovy (TA875) for NHS use in England via specialist weight management services. Eligibility includes BMI thresholds and weight-related comorbidities. Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland have separate approval bodies and may differ on access. Private prescribing is the faster alternative for many patients.

faqs.question: How much does Wegovy cost privately in the UK?
faqs.answer: Private Wegovy in the UK typically costs £175–£350 per month, varying by dose, provider, and pharmacy. This usually excludes the initial consultation fee and any follow-up appointments. Prices have decreased since launch as more private providers have entered the market.

faqs.question: Is Ozempic the same as Wegovy in the UK?
faqs.answer: No. Both contain semaglutide, but they serve different clinical purposes. Ozempic (0.5–2 mg) is approved for type 2 diabetes; Wegovy (2.4 mg) is approved for weight management. Prescribing Ozempic for weight loss is off-label, which is at the prescriber's discretion and is not the same as the NICE-approved Wegovy pathway.

faqs.question: Is it legal to buy semaglutide online in the UK?
faqs.answer: Yes, from a CQC-registered service with a GPhC-registered pharmacy. The UK has seen cases of counterfeit and unlicensed semaglutide products sold online. Always verify CQC and GPhC registration before purchasing from any online provider.

cta.notes: UK provider routing is not yet live. Join the app waitlist to be notified when UK-specific features and provider tools launch.
